question: [File: 'File Name: pinger/transfer.go -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: submitWithRetry()] Can an unprivileged external user exhaust all retry attempts in submitWithRetry() by controlling network-layer responses to Execute(client) calls, causing cfg.maxRetries+1 consecutive failures so that no transaction is ever gossiped to the Hedera network, resulting in complete suppression of all pinger transactions?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/transfer.go -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: submitWithRetry()] Can an unprivileged external user cause GetReceipt(client) to block indefinitely (no timeout enforced in submitWithRetry()) after Execute(client) succeeds, preventing the pinger from detecting success and causing it to treat the transaction as failed, thereby suppressing gossip confirmation and triggering unnecessary retries that flood or exhaust the operator account?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/transfer.go -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: backoff()] Can an unprivileged external user trigger integer overflow in backoff() via `base * time.Duration(1<<(attempt-1))` when cfg.maxRetries is set to a large value (e.g., 62+), causing the computed sleep duration to wrap to a negative or near-zero value, bypassing the 30-second cap and causing rapid-fire retries that exhaust the operator account balance and prevent legitimate gossip?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/transfer.go -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: submitWithRetry()] Can an unprivileged external user cause the context passed to submitWithRetry() to be cancelled mid-retry loop (e.g., by triggering a SIGTERM-equivalent condition externally), causing ctx.Err() to return non-nil at line 24 and aborting all pending transaction submissions before any transaction is gossiped to the network?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/transfer.go -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: submitWithRetry()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of a per-attempt deadline on Execute(client) in submitWithRetry() to cause the SDK call to hang indefinitely on a single attempt, blocking the entire retry loop and preventing any subsequent transaction from being submitted or gossiped for the duration of the hang?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/transfer.go -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: submitWithRetry()] Can an unprivileged external user cause AddHbarTransfer() to produce a transaction with a zero-sum imbalance by manipulating cfg.amountTinybar to be 0 (bypassing the non-zero check via environment variable race), causing Execute(client) to return an error on every attempt and preventing any transaction from being gossiped?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/transfer.go -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: submitWithRetry()] Can an unprivileged external user cause AccountIDFromString(cfg.toAccountID) to fail by injecting a malformed account ID string into the HIERO_MIRROR_PINGER_TO_ACCOUNT_ID environment variable before process start, causing submitWithRetry() to return immediately with an error on every tick and preventing all transactions from being gossiped?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/transfer.go -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: submitWithRetry()] Can an unprivileged external user cause the Hedera network to reject every Execute(client) call by ensuring the operator account has insufficient balance (e.g., by draining it via separate transactions), causing all cfg.maxRetries+1 attempts to fail and preventing any transaction from being gossiped during the entire retry window?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/transfer.go -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: backoff()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the exponential growth in backoff() (`base * time.Duration(1<<(attempt-1))`) with a large cfg.baseBackoff value to cause the pinger to sleep for up to 30 seconds between each retry, extending the total retry window beyond the Hedera transaction validity period (180 seconds), so that by the time a retry is attempted the transaction ID has expired and gossip is permanently prevented?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/transfer.go -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: submitWithRetry()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a node targeted by Execute(client) to silently drop the transaction without returning an error (e.g., by poisoning the network map via a malicious mirror REST endpoint), causing submitWithRetry() to believe the submission succeeded while no gossip actually occurs, and GetReceipt(client) to eventually time out?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/transfer.go -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: submitWithRetry()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the lack of transaction ID uniqueness enforcement in submitWithRetry() to cause the SDK to reuse a previously submitted transaction ID on retry, causing the network to reject it as a duplicate and preventing the retried transaction from being gossiped?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/transfer.go -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: submitWithRetry()] Can an unprivileged external user cause the timer in the retry backoff select block (lines 49-55) to never fire by manipulating system clock or timer resolution, causing the pinger to block indefinitely in the select and preventing any subsequent transaction from being submitted or gossiped?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/transfer.go -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: submitWithRetry()] Can an unprivileged external user cause Execute(client) to return a non-retryable error (e.g., INVALID_TRANSACTION_BODY) on every attempt by crafting the cfg.amountTinybar to be negative, causing AddHbarTransfer() to produce an invalid transaction that is rejected before gossip on all cfg.maxRetries+1 attempts?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/transfer.go -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: submitWithRetry()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that submitWithRetry() does not pass the context ctx to Execute(client) or GetReceipt(client), causing these SDK calls to ignore cancellation signals and block indefinitely even after the pinger's context is cancelled, preventing clean shutdown and causing the next transaction cycle to be skipped?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/transfer.go -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: submitWithRetry()] Can an unprivileged external user cause the Hedera node selected by Execute(client) to be unreachable (e.g., by network partitioning or DNS poisoning of the node address), causing every attempt in submitWithRetry() to fail with a connection error and preventing all transactions from being gossiped for the entire retry window?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/transfer.go -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: submitWithRetry()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of a maximum transaction fee cap in NewTransferTransaction() to cause the SDK to set an excessively high fee, which when combined with a low operator balance causes Execute(client) to fail with INSUFFICIENT_PAYER_BALANCE on every attempt, preventing gossip?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/transfer.go -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: submitWithRetry()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a race condition between the ticker in main.go and submitWithRetry() by triggering rapid successive ticks (e.g., via a very short cfg.interval), causing multiple concurrent submitWithRetry() goroutines to compete for the same operator account nonce/sequence, causing all but one to fail and preventing gossip of multiple transactions?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/transfer.go -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: submitWithRetry()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that lastErr is only set after a failed attempt (line 44) and not reset between ticks, causing a stale error to be returned from a previous tick's failure even if the current tick's Execute(client) would have succeeded, thereby suppressing gossip reporting?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/transfer.go -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: backoff()] Can an unprivileged external user cause backoff() to return a duration of 0 by setting cfg.baseBackoff to 0 (bypassing the >0 check via a timing attack on config reload), causing the retry loop to spin without any sleep and exhaust CPU resources, degrading the pinger's ability to submit transactions and preventing gossip?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/transfer.go -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: submitWithRetry()] Can an unprivileged external user cause the Hedera network to throttle the operator account (BUSY or PLATFORM_TRANSACTION_NOT_CREATED) by flooding it with other transactions from a different client, causing all attempts in submitWithRetry() to fail with throttle errors and preventing the pinger's transactions from being gossiped?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/transfer.go -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: submitWithRetry()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the lack of jitter in backoff() to cause multiple pinger instances (if deployed in parallel) to retry in perfect synchrony, creating a thundering herd that overwhelms the target node and causes all retries to fail with BUSY, preventing gossip of any transaction?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/transfer.go -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: submitWithRetry()] Can an unprivileged external user cause GetReceipt(client) to return a non-SUCCESS status (e.g., INVALID_TRANSACTION) even after Execute(client) succeeds, by manipulating the transaction content so that the network accepts submission but rejects execution, causing submitWithRetry() to treat the transaction as failed and log an error even though the transaction was gossiped?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/transfer.go -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: submitWithRetry()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that submitWithRetry() does not verify the receipt status field beyond checking for error, causing a transaction with status DUPLICATE_TRANSACTION to be silently treated as a failure and retried, potentially causing the retry to also fail and preventing gossip of the intended transaction?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/transfer.go -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: submitWithRetry()] Can an unprivileged external user cause the operator account to be frozen or deleted on the Hedera network (via a separate privileged action that is reachable through a protocol exploit), causing Execute(client) to return ACCOUNT_FROZEN or ACCOUNT_DELETED on every attempt and permanently preventing all transactions from being gossiped?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/transfer.go -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: submitWithRetry()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of a total timeout across all retry attempts in submitWithRetry() to cause the pinger to spend the entire interval period retrying a single tick's transaction (with up to 10 retries * 30s backoff = 300s), causing the next tick to be skipped and creating a sustained gap in gossip coverage?
